text.line: LOOKS LIKE WE'VE GOT THE CHECKLIST DONE, WE CAN PROBABLY HEAD TO THE BOARD AND CHECK WHAT WE NEED FOR THE NEXT FLOOR.

intro.line: YOU FIND YOURSELF IN A SMALL ROOM WITH A DOOR TO THE NORTH AND A DOOR TO THE SOUTH.
YOU ARE IN A SMALL LOUNGE ROOM WITH A FEW CHAIRS AND A TABLE. A CHECKLIST SITS ON A CORKBOARD ON THE WALL.
YOU ARE IN A SPACIOUS CORRIDOR WITH LOFTY LIGHTS HANGING ABOVE. THERE IS AN ELEVATOR TO THE SOUTH AND A DOORWAY TO THE EAST AND WEST.
YOU FIND YOURSELF IN A LARGE ROOM WITH DOZENS OF BLINKING TERMINALS SURROUNDING YOU.
THE ROOM WHIRS WITH THE SOUND OF SEVERAL LARGE FILTRATION UNITS AROUND YOU, DUST HANGING IN THE AIR.
YOU ARE IN A BRIGHT ROOM FILLED WITH ELECTRICAL EQUIPMENT AND SEVERAL LARGE GENERATORS.
YOU ARE IN A SMALL STEEL ELEVATOR WITH THREE BUTTONS, ONE FOR EACH FLOOR OF THE FACILITY.

text.line: THE ROOM IS LARGELY EMPTY, WITH A SINGLE DARK TERMINAL AND A THICK CABLE DISCONNECTED ON THE FLOOR.
THERE IS A MAP OF THE FACILITY ON THE WALL, SHOWING THE GENERAL LAYOUT OF THE FACILITY.
THERE IS A DOOR SOUTH LEADING TO THE FACILITY LOUNGE, AND A DOOR LEADING EAST TO A LARGE CORRIDOR.
A DETAILED MAP SHOWS THE GENERAL LAYOUT OF THE FACILITY, SPLIT INTO THREE FLOORS.
YOU ARE CURRENTLY IN ROOM A1 ON THE FIRST FLOOR.
PROCESSING DATA....
MAP DATA RETRIEVED SUCCESSFULLY.
A1: BOOT ROOM. A2: FACILITY LOUNGE. A3: FLOOR ONE CORRIDOR. A4: CENTRAL DATABASE. A5: AIR FILTRATION CENTER. A6: GENERATOR ROOM. A7: ELEVATOR.
THE CORKBOARD IS COVERED WITH NOTES AND MEMOS DETAILING OFFICE ETIQUETTE AND DAILY OPERATIONS, ALONGSIDE YOUR MONTHLY CHECKLIST.
A VENDING MACHINE SITS IN THE CORNER OF THE ROOM.
THERE IS A SMALL COFFEE MAKER ON THE TABLE, ALONGSIDE SEVERAL MAGAZINES.
THE TABLE IS COVERED IN A THICK LAYER OF DUST. SOMEONE SHOULD PROBABLY CLEAN THAT.
THE TABLE AND COFFEEMAKER ARE SPOTLESS. YOU PAT YOURSELF ON THE BACK.
THE CHAIRS ARE WORN AND TATTERED, WITH TEARS IN THE FABRIC AND EXPOSED FOAM.
THERE IS A DOOR LEADING NORTH BACK TO THE BOOT ROOM.
PROCESSING CHECKLIST DATA...
THE CHECKLIST IS THOROUGH AND DETAILED. BEST TO TAKE IT ONE FLOOR AT A TIME.
CURRENT FACILITY LOGS: FLOOR 1 STATUS
[ ] CLEAN FACILITY LOUNGE: PENDING
[X] CLEAN FACILITY LOUNGE: COMPLETE
[ ] ENSURE SYSTEMWIDE TERMINAL FUNCTIONALITY: PENDING
[X] ENSURE SYSTEMWIDE TERMINAL FUNCTIONALITY: COMPLETE
[ ] REPLACE AIR FILTERS / MONITOR OXYGEN: PENDING
[X] REPLACE AIR FILTERS / MONITOR OXYGEN: COMPLETE
[ ] MONITOR GENERATOR PERFORMANCE / BATTERY: PENDING
[X] MONITOR GENERATOR PERFORMANCE / BATTERY: COMPLETE
AFTER COMPLETION OF FIRST FLOOR DUTIES LOG PERFORMANCE INTO THE TERMINAL AND MOVE ON TO FLOOR TWO. THE REMAINDER OF THE CHECKLIST WILL BECOME AVAILABLE AS NEEDED.
YOU CAN RETURN TO THE CHECKLIST AT ANY POINT TO SEE YOUR PROGRESS.
THE VENDING MACHINE IS FULLY STOCKED, ALL CLEAR HERE.
NONE OF THEM LOOK PARTICULARLY APPETIZING ANYWAYS, TRUST ME NOT WORTH A DESCRIPTION.
YOU GRAB A NEARBY RAG AND THOROUGHLY WIPE THE TABLE AND COFFEE MAKER DOWN, ORGANIZING THE MAGAZINES INTO A STACK.
WHILE YOU'RE AT IT, YOU ALSO WIPE DOWN THE CHAIRS, REMOVING SOME OF THE DUST AND DEBRIS.
YOU GRAB A NEARBY BROOM AND SWEEP THE FLOOR, MAKING THE ROOM FEEL MUCH CLEANER A LITTLE MORE LIVABLE FOR THE STAFF.
A SENSE OF PRIDE WELLS UP IN YOU AS YOU STEP BACK AND ADMIRE YOUR WORK.
MUCH BETTER.
THE WALLS OF THE CORRIDOR ARE A STARK WHITE, WITH DOORS TO BOTH SIDES OF YOU AND AN ELEVATOR TO THE SOUTH. ON THE NORTH SIDE, THERE IS A LARGE WINDOW.

YOU APPROACH THE WINDOW AND LOOK OUT INTO THE DARKNESS BELOW. IT'S A BREATHTAKING SIGHT, A DEEP SILO STRETCHING FAR INTO THE VOID, DIM LIGHTS EMBOSSED WITHIN THE WALL GIVING YOU A SLIGHT GLIMPSE AT WHAT LIES WITHIN.
MISSILES. TOO MANY TO COUNT, STACKED SIDE BY SIDE LIKE OMINOUS SENTINELS. I SUPPOSE THAT ANSWERS WHAT THE FACILITY IS FOR I GUESS.
IT'S TRULY A MAGESTIC SIGHT THOUGH, REGARDLESS OF HOW UNNERVING IT IS. TRUST ME, IT WAS WORTH THE LOOK.
NOT TERRIBLY MUCH ELSE TO DESCRIBE HERE, THERE'S A CORRIDOR TO THE WEST AND A DOOR TO THE SOUTH. THE TERMINALS CONTINUE TO BLINK BLANKLY AT YOU, WAITING FOR SOME KIND OF INPUT.

YOU APPROACH THE NEAREST TERMINAL AND TAP AT IT, THE SCREEN BOUNCING TO LIFE SEEMINGLY ECSTATIC TO FINALLY RECIEVE SOME ATTENTION. 
AN INSIGNIA OF A STAR WITHIN A GEAR SPINS IN THE CORNER WHILE THE INTRO TEXT ROLLS OVER THE SCREEN.
K-LEVEL BIOS V.4.23 - INITIALIZING...

Hello brother and welcome to the Allied Front Operations System (c). 
Please login to continue to the primary interface.
YOU TYPE IN THE CREDENTIALS TO YOUR ACCOUNT LIKE SECOND NATURE, THE TERMINAL HESITATING FOR A MOMENT BEFORE PROCESSING YOUR REQUEST.
Welcome back [[FACILITY MAINTENANCE OPERATOR]], please select an option to perform your duties.
THE TERMINAL NOW DISPLAYS SEVERAL OPTIONS FOR YOU TO CHOOSE FROM, THE MOST INTERESTING BEING FACILITY HISTORY. PROBABLY WORTH A PEEK.
RUNNING SYSTEM DIAGNOSTICS...

[SYSTEM]: CHECKING CORE MEMORY... OK 
[SYSTEM]: CHECKING PROCESSOR FUNCTIONALITY... OK 
[SYSTEM]: CHECKING NETWORK CONNECTIVITY... ERROR 
NETWORK CONNECTION LOST. PLEASE SEND A MAINTENTANCE REQUEST TO YOUR NEAREST IT SPECIALIST. 
[SYSTEM]: SCANNING LOGIC GATES... OK 
[SYSTEM]: VALIDATING ROBOTICS WIRELESS CONNECTION... OK
[SYSTEM]: ENSURING SYSTEM RADIATION INTEGRITY... ERROR#&*$*@(&%&*@
 [SYSTEM]: DIAGNOSTICS COMPLETE. SYSTEMS WITHIN NORMAL PARAMETERS.
WELL, MILDLY CONCERNING BUT NOTHING TO WORRY ABOUT AS FAR AS OUR RESPONSIBILITIES GO. I THINK I'M SAFE TO CHECK THAT OFF THE LIST.
ACCESSING FACILITY STATUS REPORTS...
FACILITY STATUS REPORTS MISSING. PLEASE CONTACT YOUR LOCAL FACILITY MAINTENANCE OPERATOR TO PERFORM MONTHLY MANUAL FACILITY MAINTENANCE CHECK AND LOG THE RESULTS.
I SUPPOSE THAT WOULD BE US. BETTER GET BACK TO WORK THEN.
ACCESSING FACILITY HISTORY LOGS...

Test Site Chelbaskia-40 is a cutting edge nuclear testing facility storing ~30% of the Allied Forces' nuclear arsenal, making it an integral part of our defensive line against the Collective Front (ten-thousand curses be to their names). 
While proudly holding the mantle of first line of defense, Chelbaskia-40 is also a cornerstone of experimental research and development for the Allied Front, with a large focus on experimental botanics, high-density fission generators, cutting-edge robotics technology, and functioning as an experimental bunker in the case of nuclear warfare. 
In the case of complete separation from the external grid Chelbaskia-40 has the resources to last ~200 years, with our fission generators capable on running in lower power mode for up to two centuries. 
Chelbaskia-40 is a model example of the superior power of Allied Forces' technology, and I hope you and your brothers are proud to be working there.
WELL, A BIT DRY BUT INTERESTING NONETHELESS. NICE TO KNOW WHERE YOU'RE WORKING I GUESS. BACK TO THE GRINDSTONE.
[A5 INSPECT RESULT]
[A6 INSPECT RESULT]
[A7 INSPECT RESULT]
